_template_instructions.description: Commercial SaaS SSP template — fill ORG_ALIAS, PLATFORM, and ORG_UUID placeholders
_template_instructions.framework: Commercial adaptation of FedRAMP SSP patterns — gov red tape removed
key_differences_from_fedramp: No JAB/PMO references — replaced with Security Team and application owner
No FIPS 199 government impact categories — replaced with SSCF RED/AMBER/GREEN sensitivity tier
No physical media / PS (personnel screening) controls — SaaS-irrelevant
No government-specific authorization boundaries — replaced with SaaS API + IdP boundary
Sensitivity tier derived from SSCF score: RED=high, AMBER=moderate, GREEN=low
Regulatory crosswalk: SOX/HIPAA/SOC2/ISO 27001/PCI DSS/GDPR (no FedRAMP-specific refs)

metadata.title: System Security Plan — ORG_ALIAS_PLACEHOLDER (PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER)
metadata.remarks: Commercial SaaS SSP for PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER deployment ORG_ALIAS_PLACEHOLDER. Generated from SSCF assessment results by scripts/gen_ssp.py. This SSP follows NIST OSCAL 1.1.2 structure adapted for commercial SaaS governance — FedRAMP-specific requirements (JAB, PMO, FIPS 199, government data categories) removed. Regulatory obligations: SOX / HIPAA / SOC2 / ISO 27001:2022 / PCI DSS v4.

parties.name: ORG_ALIAS_PLACEHOLDER Application Owner
parties.remarks: Fill with actual owner name and contact.

import-profile.remarks: Platform-specific SSCF sub-profile (SBS for Salesforce, WSCC for Workday).

system-characteristics.description: PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER SaaS deployment for ORG_ALIAS_PLACEHOLDER. Assessed against SSCF v1.0 controls. Sensitivity tier: SENSITIVITY_TIER_PLACEHOLDER.

props.name: regulatory-obligations
props.value: SOX, HIPAA, SOC2, ISO 27001:2022, PCI DSS v4, GDPR (as applicable)

information-types.title: Business Data — PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER
information-types.description: Data processed by ORG_ALIAS_PLACEHOLDER's PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER deployment. Classification per organization data classification policy.

authorization-boundary.description: Authorization boundary includes: (1) PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER SaaS tenant accessed via web browser and REST API; (2) enterprise IdP (Okta/Azure AD/Ping) providing authentication; (3) SIEM receiving forwarded audit logs; (4) CI/CD pipeline running automated assessments. Excludes: vendor-managed infrastructure and underlying cloud platform.

network-architecture.description: All connections to PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER use HTTPS/TLS 1.2+. Authentication via SAML 2.0 SSO through enterprise IdP. API access via OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens. No plaintext connections to external endpoints.

data-flow.description: Data flow: User browser → IdP (SAML) → PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER SaaS. API integrations → PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER REST/Tooling API (OAuth 2.0). Audit logs → SIEM (HTTPS/TCP syslog). Assessment pipeline → read-only API queries (no writes).

components.title: PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER SaaS Platform
components.purpose: Core business application providing PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER capabilities.

components.title: Enterprise Identity Provider
components.description: Enterprise IdP providing SAML 2.0 SSO and SCIM provisioning for PLATFORM_PLACEHOLDER.

control-implementation.description: Control implementation inherited from SSCF platform assessment. See component definition for per-control collection methods and evidence specifications.
